natural_occurrence.description: Synthetic antioxidant with longer alkyl chain than propyl gallate, providing greater fat solubility. Derived from gallic acid (natural) esterified with octanol.

manufacturing.description: Produced by esterifying gallic acid with octanol (octyl alcohol). Greater fat solubility than propyl gallate due to longer carbon chain (8 carbons). Linear formula: 3,4,5-(HO)₃C₆H₂CO₂(CH₂)₇CH₃, Molecular weight: 282.33.

other_uses.cosmetics: Historical use in cosmetics (now largely discontinued)

eu.conditions: REMOVED from EU approved additives list in 2018 due to persistent safety concerns
eu.legal_reference: Previously in Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, now removed

usa.notes: Previously approved with limits; current use declining after EU removal

australia_nz.notes: Approved but use declining after EU removal

regulatory_history.event: SCF establishes group ADI of 0-0.2 mg/kg bw for three gallates (E310, E311, E312)
JECFA declines to establish individual ADI for octyl gallate - early warning sign of safety concerns
EU removes E311 (octyl gallate) from approved additives list along with E312 (dodecyl gallate)

commonly_found_in: Historical use in vegetable oils and fats
NOTE: Use significantly restricted or eliminated in most jurisdictions after EU removal

adi_context.source_note: No ADI established. JECFA's decision NOT to establish an ADI for octyl gallate in 1996 was an early warning sign. EU removed authorization in 2018.

fun_facts.title: Longer Chain, Better Solubility
fun_facts.description: The longer octyl chain (8 carbons vs. 3 in propyl) gives octyl gallate greater fat solubility, making it more effective in pure oil systems than propyl gallate.

fun_facts.title: The Fallen Brother
fun_facts.description: Once used alongside propyl and dodecyl gallate, it was removed from EU's approved list in 2018 while propyl gallate survived - a cautionary tale about long-term safety evaluation.

fun_facts.title: Safety Uncertainty
fun_facts.description: JECFA's decision NOT to establish an ADI for octyl gallate in 1996 was an early warning sign - when regulators can't set a safe intake level, it raises red flags.

fun_facts.title: Emulsion Partitioning
fun_facts.description: Its intermediate chain length affects how it distributes between oil and water phases in emulsions like mayonnaise, influencing where its antioxidant activity is most effective.

fun_facts.title: Regulatory Lesson
fun_facts.description: Investigation of potential genotoxic effects was among the concerns that led to its removal - a reminder that food additive safety evaluation is ongoing, not static.
